solution:
def smallestRepunitDivByK(k: int) -> int:
    n = 1
    while k > 1:
        n *= 10
        k //= 10
    return n // k

efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(log(k))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The algorithm uses a while loop to incrementally increase the number n by multiplying it by 10 and dividing k by 10 until k becomes 1. At this point, n is the smallest positive integer divisible by k and only containing the digit 1. The solution returns n divided by k. The time complexity is O(log(k)) because the loop runs log(k) times on average. The space complexity is O(1) because the algorithm uses a constant amount of space. The energy implications are low because the algorithm does not require any additional resources beyond the standard Python operations.
